data.title: Is XRP About To Shock The Market? Analyst Says $110 Is Possible
data.description:
Ripple’s XRP, one of the top-performing cryptos last year, is trading between $2.15 and $2.30. The token’s current price is a far cry from its 52-week high of $3.38 last January 17th, which was spurred by the industry’s excitement over US President Donald Trump’s reelection. 

Although XRP joins the downtrend of the broader crypto market, a few commentators and analysts expect that Ripple is due for a major rally. According to crypto analyst Egrag Crypto, Ripple’s XRP is set for another price run after it compares the asset’s current Elliot Wave structure with its 2017 fractals.

XRP Continues To Weather The Storm

Egrag Crypto’s latest price analysis and projection for XRP come as the altcoin and the broader crypto market slump. The Ripple’s coin is holding steady above the critical $2 price support, which suggests it follows the broad five-wave Elliott Wave chart. Traditionally, assets replicating the Elliott Wave structure often incur significant price action.

EGRAG CRYPTO
@egragcrypto

#XRP = Thread (1/7) #XRP: Double Digits This Cycle, Triple Digits Next!

The thread below about #XRP was shared in the Subscribers section on February 12, 2025.

We’ve built together the Full Elliot Wave Count to assess our next Targets:

Take an in-depth look at it! … pic.twitter.com/NKv00Y5MZD

Mar 12, 2025

Based on Egrag’s assessment, XRP is currently in the second wave of the Elliot Wave structure, which is traditionally defined by high price volatility and corrections.

Traders And Investors Must Watch Out For The Larger Wave Structure

In a Twitter/X post, Egrag explained that XRP is in its corrective wave two and is primed for a price run to double digits or even over $100.

The analysts elaborated that a Wave 2 often retraces a part of Wave 1, usually matching 50%, 76% or even 85.4% of the initial movement. Ripple’s XRP, he shares, is solidifying its hold in the correction phase.

The asset faces bearish pressure, and the market can expect a double bottom soon before it moves into its Wave 3. EGRAG warns that Wave 3 is the most aggressive part of the cycle, with more volatility.Can XRP Hit $100 Or More?

Previously, XRP’s Wave 1 pattern submitted a huge 733% increase in price. And by using the Elliot Wave extension formula, the popular analyst projects that XRP’s Wave 3 can extend by 1.618x the gain of the first wave. Using this calculation, Egrag offers a potential surge of 1,185x, translating to XRP’s price range between $22 to $24.

Once XRP hits the peak of Wave 3, the fourth wave and a price correction follows. For this wage, the retracement level ranges between 14% to 38.2% of Wave 3. If this happens, Egrag predicts that XRP’s price can drop to $8.

Wave 5 of the Elliot Wave follows, where price can be predicted using three methods. First, if XRP’s price grows between 1.236% and 1.618%, the asset’s price can hit between $32 and $48. Second, if Wave 5 replicates Wave 1, the price can be between $60 and $70. Third, if there’s a 61.8% extension of the movements of Waves 1 and 3, then there’s a chance that XRP’s price can hit $100.

Featured image from Medium, chart from TradingView

data.title: Nongshim's Rising Cost Pressures Likely to Weigh on Profitability — Market Talk
data.description: Nongshim's rising cost pressures are likely to weigh on its profitability, Daiwa Capital analysts Youngho Jang and Mike Oh write in a note. The South Korean food company, famous for its Shin Ramyun instant-noodle brand, is projected to see a 20% jump in marketing expenses and a 12% increase in wages and employee benefits this year, they say. They cut their 2025 EPS forecast for Nongshim by 14%. They also trim profit-margin estimates for the company despite its recent average-selling-price increase. Daiwa downgrades its rating on the stock to outperform from buy, cutting its target price by 14% to KRW420,000. Shares are 0.1% lower at KRW381,500. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Dollar holds firm, euro drifts as global trade tensions escalate
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. dollar holds firm amid trade tensions with EU
  • Euro on defensive after sliding further from 5-month peak
  • Japanese spring wage negotiation results in focus

The U.S. dollar was broadly firmer on Friday with the euro pulling further away from a five-month peak as markets grappled with escalating global trade tensions and risks of a sharp economic downturn.

Sowing more volatility across markets, U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to hit Europe with a 200% tariff on wine, cognac and other alcohol imports.

The escalating tensions between the traditional allies came after the EU bloc announced plans to impose levies on American whiskey and other products next month, which itself was a response to Trump's 25% tariffs on steel and aluminium imports that took effect earlier this week.

The intensifying global trade skirmish has fuelled uncertainty and fears about a potentially sharp economic slowdown, with the S&P 500 tumbling into correction territory on Thursday as investors piled into U.S. Treasuries and other safe haven assets.

The euro edged down to $1.0847 after sliding further off Tuesday's five-month peak the previous day as the EU-U.S. trade spat rattled markets and Germany struggled to pass a massive spending proposal.

Hopes of an imminent ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia were also fading as Moscow said it supported the U.S. proposal but suggested it would need some serious reworking.

The softer euro helped lift the greenback further away from Tuesday's trough of 103.21, its lowest since mid-October, though concerns simmered about the outlook for the U.S. and broader global economy.

"I think the million-dollar question with whatever asset class you're looking at is...where do we start to find the news that's going to turn around risk sentiment? And at this point in time, it's not clear," said Tony Sycamore, a market analyst at IG.

Since climbing to a six-month peak back in January, the dollar has fallen more than 5%, giving up major ground against the euro, sterling, and yen as the U.S. exceptionalism narrative started to fray.

A potential U.S. government shutdown added to uncertainties, although top U.S. Senate Democrat Chuck Schumer on Thursday said he would vote to advance a Republican stopgap funding bill, signalling that his party would provide the votes to avert a shutdown.

Elsewhere, the pound drifted around $1.2945 ahead of the release of gross domestic product figures for January, after falling in the previous session from Wednesday's high of $1.2990, its highest against the dollar since early November.

WAGE RESULTS AWAITED

The yen walked back some of its gains on Friday, with the dollar trading at 148.32 yen, up 0.35%.

The Japanese currency strengthened to as high as 146.545 per dollar earlier this week, buoyed by safe-haven bids and bets that the Bank of Japan (BOJ) will raise interest rates again later this year.

Market players had their eyes on first round results of spring wage negotiations expected sometime later in the day which are expected to encourage the BOJ to continue normalizing monetary policy.

Economists and markets see the central bank standing pat at its meeting next week as policymakers gauge global risks.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, rose 0.1% to 103.95 after two straight days of gains.

The Canadian dollar wallowed at 1.4440 per U.S. dollar, with Canada caught in the tariff crossfire.

The risk-sensitive Australian dollar steadied at $0.6284 after slipping on Thursday, while the New Zealand dollar was up 0.1% at $0.5702.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin was up 1.32% at $81,410.36.

data.title: ESR REIT Prices SG$125 Million Worth of Bonds
data.description:
ESR REIT priced SG$125 million of 5.75% subordinated perpetual bonds, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The bonds will be issued under the SG$750 million multicurrency debt issuance program.

DBS Bank and Oversea-Chinese Banking have been appointed as the joint lead managers for the bonds.

Proceeds from the issuance will be used for refinancing or repayment of existing borrowings, along with general working capital and capital expenditure requirement.

data.title: Gold Circuit Electronics' Business Outlook Appears Positive — Market Talk
data.description: Gold Circuit Electronics' business outlook appears positive, thanks to strong revenue prospects for application-specific integrated circuit AI servers and specifications upgrade trends, Daiwa Capital Markets' Sheng Cheng says in a research report. The total addressable market for ASIC AI servers' printed circuit boards is expected to reach NT$20 billion-NT$25 billion in 2025 and the Taiwanese company is likely to have a 25%-30% market share, the analyst estimates. This would probably contribute NT$5 billion-NT$6 billion in revenue for the PCB manufacturer. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to NT$290.00 from NT$270.00 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 0.2% higher at NT$217.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0203 GMT - Gold Circuit Electronics' business outlook appears positive, thanks to strong revenue prospects for application-specific integrated circuit AI servers and specifications upgrade trends, Daiwa Capital Markets' Sheng Cheng says in a research report. The total addressable market for ASIC AI servers' printed circuit boards is expected to reach NT$20 billion-NT$25 billion in 2025 and the Taiwanese company is likely to have a 25%-30% market share, the analyst estimates. This would probably contribute NT$5 billion-NT$6 billion in revenue for the PCB manufacturer. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to NT$290.00 from NT$270.00 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 0.2% higher at NT$217.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0201 GMT - Nongshim's rising cost pressures are likely to weigh on its profitability, Daiwa Capital analysts Youngho Jang and Mike Oh write in a note. The South Korean food company, famous for its Shin Ramyun instant-noodle brand, is projected to see a 20% jump in marketing expenses and a 12% increase in wages and employee benefits this year, they say. They cut their 2025 EPS forecast for Nongshim by 14%. They also trim profit-margin estimates for the company despite its recent average-selling-price increase. Daiwa downgrades its rating on the stock to outperform from buy, cutting its target price by 14% to KRW420,000. Shares are 0.1% lower at KRW381,500. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0159 GMT - Chinese shares are higher in early trade, supported by consumer and insurance stocks. Investors await China's February economic data due Monday, including industrial output and retail sales. Gainers in the session include Yonghui Superstores, which is up 1.7%, Kweichow Moutai up 0.9%, China Life Insurance adding 0.6% and Ping An Insurance 1.1% higher. Steel stocks are broadly lower, with Baoshan Iron & Steel down 1.6%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is 0.3% higher at 3369.64, the Shenzhen Composite Index is up 0.1% and the ChiNext Price Index rises 0.7%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0157 GMT - The outlook for Malaysia's utilities sector remains positive, driven by rising electricity demand, data-center expansion, and strong renewable-energy growth, RHB IB analysts Sean Lim and Miza Izaimi say in a note. Tenaga Nasional guided for steady progress in data-center projects and inquiries despite U.S. chip export restrictions, as most projects are non-AI related and primarily backed by China or Chinese-backed entities, they note. Solar power growth also remains robust, with large-scale projects rolling out, boosting contractors' order books, they add. RHB maintains an overweight rating on Malaysia's utilities sector, pegging Tenaga Nasional, YTL Power International and Solarvest as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0157 GMT - The outlook for Malaysia's utilities sector remains positive, driven by rising electricity demand, data-center expansion, and strong renewable-energy growth, RHB IB analysts Sean Lim and Miza Izaimi say in a note. Tenaga Nasional guided for steady progress in data-center projects and inquiries despite U.S. chip export restrictions, as most projects are non-AI related and primarily backed by China or Chinese-backed entities, they note. Solar power growth also remains robust, with large-scale projects rolling out, boosting contractors' order books, they add. RHB maintains an overweight rating on Malaysia's utilities sector, pegging Tenaga Nasional, YTL Power International and Solarvest as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0151 GMT - Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index rises 0.4% to 23548.43, led by gains in consumer and tech stocks. Markets remain firmly focused on trade tariff news, and sentiment towards risk assets are starting to weaken as U.S. tariffs start to take effect against China, Mexico and Canada, and other metal imports, the UOB Global Economics & Markets Research team writes in a note. Among the gainers, China Mengniu Dairy rises 4.7%, Baidu gains 2.8% and Alibaba Group is 1.8% higher. The decliners include CK Hutchison, which is down 5.9%, WH Group off 3.4% and AIA Group, which is 2.4% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index is down 0.1% at 5741.10. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0132 GMT - DFI Retail Group looks positioned for earnings growth in 2025, UOB Kay Hian's Adrian Loh says in a research report. For 2025, the Asian retailer guided for 2% revenue growth and projected underlying profit of US$230 million-US$270 million, the analyst notes. The Singapore-listed company expects to generate this growth from its health & beauty and convenience segments and from optimizing its product mix to improve margins, the analyst says. Also, the company disclosed it'll seek to monetize data from its "yuu" platform and advertising opportunities within its supermarkets, among other initiatives. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to US$2.80 from US$2.57 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are unchanged at US$2.25. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0120 GMT - Mr. D.I.Y. Group (M) could face a potential exclusion from Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index in the semiannual review this June, Affin Hwang IB analyst Low Jing Yuan says in a note. The company's market capitalization ranks 34th after a recent selloff, falling outside the top-30 threshold for index inclusion, he says. Shares are 29% lower year-to-date. Its aggressive store expansion appears to have reached a saturation point, with diminishing revenue contribution per outlet and rising operating costs likely to pressure margins. The weak 3Q and 4Q results last year reflected these challenges, he says. Affin Hwang cuts the stock's target price to MYR1.10 from MYR1.25, on downside risks from slowing growth and potential index removal, and keeps a sell rating. Shares are 2.2% lower at MYR1.32. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0112 GMT - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.2% lower to 3831.46, tracking Wall Street's losses overnight. The main theme has been risk-off, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. There are worries over further escalation in the trade war, the analysts say, noting President Trump's threat to impose 200% tariff on U.S. imports of wine, champagne and other alcoholic beverages from the EU. Among worst performers on the benchmark index, Sembcorp Industries falls 2.6%, Mapletree Logistics Trust loses 0.8%, and OCBC is down 0.7%. Meanwhile, Wilmar International rises 0.6% and index heavyweight DBS adds 0.4%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0107 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 1.0% lower at 1495.59. With global markets unsettled ahead of the coming reciprocal tariffs, profit-taking in the local bourse may occur after Thursday's rebound, Apex Securities says in a note. Persistent foreign fund outflows also add to the volatility, the brokerage reckons. It maintains a defensive stance, advising a wait-and-see approach until trade policy clarity emerges post-April. Among the decliners, Sunway Bhd. is down 2.3% and Public Bank is 2.2% lower. Meanwhile, Maxis Bhd. adds 0.6% and YTL Power International is 0.3% higher. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0016 GMT - Japanese stocks are lower in early trade as concerns about U.S. tariffs weigh. Tech and chemical stocks are leading the declines. Recruit Holdings is down 4.4% and Shin-Etsu Chemical is 1.5% lower. USD/JPY is at 147.88, compared with 147.64 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock-market close. Investors are paying attention to developments related to U.S. tariffs and Japanese monetary policy. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.1% at 36742.68. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0015 GMT - Australian lithium miner Liontown Resources delivered an in-line result, with revenue and cash flow largely pre-reported, say Barrenjoey analysts. First-half underlying Ebitda of A$66.0 million was well above of Barrenjoey and consensus estimates "given LTR took the approach of capitalizing all costs over and above revenue to effectively create a nil gross margin," the analysts say. Commercial production at the Kathleen Valley processing plant was declared effective Jan. 1, and capitalization of costs will stop from that date, they say. Depreciation was higher-than-anticipated, but net debt was as expected, say the analysts. They continue to expect Liontown will meet 2H FY25 guidance. Liontown is up 0.4% at A$0.6175. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Galan Lithium Posts Narrower Fiscal H1 Earnings, Income Declines
data.description:
Galan Lithium reported Friday a fiscal first-half loss of AU$0.0091 per basic share, compared with a loss of AU$0.0131 a year earlier.

Other income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$175,483, compared with AU$684,996 million a year earlier.

The company's shares rose almost 2% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Malaysia Equities Market Investors May Remain Defensive Amid Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's equity market investors may remain defensive amid uncertainty over U.S. reciprocal tariff decisions on April 2, CIMB Securities analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia say in a note. Large foreign outflows, driven by risk aversion and weak 4Q 2024 corporate earnings, have pressured the KLCI, they note. The index is 8.6% lower year-to-date at 1500.65. Foreign shareholding stands at 19.6% as of end-February, just above the record low of 19.4%, they flag. Catalysts such as easing trade tensions, a stronger ringgit, improved corporate earnings, or government initiatives are needed to drive a rebound, they add. CIMB highlights Genting Malaysia, Sime Darby, Malayan Banking, Gamuda, and RHB Bank as key picks for their large-cap status and high dividend yields. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Rising Iron Ore Price Lifts Iron Miners' Share Prices
data.description:
The share prices of iron ore mining firms have risen as iron ore prices trend upwards.

Iron ore clocked in $101.44 per tonne on Wednesday, up 0.4% on a daily basis and 0.7% on a weekly basis. Prices in Chinese yuan per tonne were up 0.7% on a daily basis and 0.5% on a weekly basis, as of Thursday.

Fortescue and BHP Group's shares each rose 2% in recent Friday trade, while Rio Tinto Group's shares gained over 1%.

data.title: Donald Trump makes Chinese stocks (somewhat) great again
data.description:
Key points:
  • China equities rally as U.S. stocks fall over Trump trade war fears
  • Global investors lured by relatively low prices, AI bets and prospect of more stimulus from Beijing
  • Some investors maintain concerns about deflationary pressures, property crisis and trade tensions

As U.S. President Donald Trump's wide-ranging trade war rouses fears of recession, global investors have found an unlikely new sanctuary: Chinese equities.

Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index - where many major Chinese companies are listed - is up 17% since Trump entered the White House in January.

That compares to an about 9% drop in the S&P 500 , which has also shed $4 trillion in market value from record highs last month.

Trump's erratic pronouncement on tariffs and moves to slash federal government spending have challenged assumptions about the appeal of U.S. stocks, which have vastly outperformed most of their global counterparts since 2021.

Investors have moved from believing in "TINA" - There is No Alternative to U.S. assets - to "TIARA" - There Is A Real Alternative - said Andy Wong, a senior Hong Kong-based executive at Pictet Asset Management.

Much of the Chinese rally has been led by technology shares that have risen 29% so far in 2025, hitting their highest level in more than three years last week. Like many of the new China equities bulls, Wong said he sees opportunities in tech, defense and consumer-facing plays.

A key reason for the optimism: Chinese stocks are cheap, trading 30% under their 2021 highs. The Hang Seng Index is priced at 7 times its projected 12-month earnings - a commonly used metric to value stocks - compared to 20 times for the S&P 500, according to LSEG data.

To be sure, Chinese equities traded cheaply for a reason. Many investors were burned after a pandemic-era government crackdown on tech stocks and questions remain over the property market and the economy. Concerns about the concentration of power in the White House are magnified in Beijing, where President Xi Jinping has no serious political opposition.

But investors see plenty of upside after a major rally in tech shares following AI startup DeepSeek's splashy debut of its R1 reasoning model. The prospect of fiscal stimulus that could lift consumption - long a drag for the Chinese economy - is another tailwind.

While some of the renewed global interest in Chinese equities has come at the expense of U.S. stocks, investors are also moving out of South Korea and India's struggling markets, according to Reuters' interviews with more than a dozen fund managers and strategists.

J.P. Morgan has seen a record amount of U.S. dollars and Chinese yuan being converted into Hong Kong dollars over the past few weeks, pointing to the force of money flowing into Hong Kong stocks, said Serene Chen, the firm's head of credit, currency & emerging market sales. She did not specify the amount or the time period.

Leo Gao at Greenwoods Asset Management said that he sold all the U.S. companies in his portfolio in early February, shortly after the emergence of DeepSeek.

The senior portfolio manager at one of Asia's largest hedge funds told investors in March that he was now especially bullish on China tech firms and other companies that cater to changing consumer habits.

GOING TO CHINA

Trump declined over the weekend to rule out the prospect of a recession for the world's largest economy, exacerbating market fears. Investors have also reacted negatively to the volatility of decision-making in the White House, which had issued last-minute delays on tariffs on Canada and Mexico.

Slowing economic data is additionally raising doubts over whether U.S. growth can outpace the rest of the rich world for much longer. U.S. equities valuations are sky-high and susceptible to any hint of trouble.

Trump has so far downplayed market turbulence and repeatedly said that "tariffs are going to make our country rich."

China, meanwhile, has been rolling out stimulus and support measures for its economy and markets. In February, Beijing held a meeting between Xi and business leaders that investors widely took as a positive signal.

"China is now the adult in the room," said Dong Chen, chief Asia strategist at Pictet Wealth Management.

Foreign-based funds invested $3.8 billion in Chinese equities in February, after three straight months of withdrawals, data from Morgan Stanley showed.

Kamal Bhatia, the New York-based chief executive of Principal Asset Management, said long-term investors like predictability.

"Even very large sophisticated investors don't want to have their investment thesis change over three years," he said.

Some investors noted the irony of a rally in the equities markets of Europe and China, which Trump has singled out as geopolitical rivals.

"The pressure that the Trump administration is putting on foreign governments... has actually, in a lot of cases, resulted in outperformance from those countries," said Ross Mayfield, a U.S.-based investment strategist at Baird.

A prospective fiscal bazooka in Europe after Trump cast doubt on his willingness to defend NATO allies has also fuelled share prices of defense companies in the region. European equities have long faced headwinds that include higher corporate tax rates, slow economic growth and a dearth of major tech firms.

"As investors adjust to the narrative change, capital will flow away from the previously crowded winners," Pictet's Wong said.

STRUCTURAL OR SHORT-TERM?

Alongside concerns around China's corporate reporting standards, the deflationary pressures and a renewed trade war with U.S. weigh on sentiment.

Stocks surged in September after Beijing unveiled stimulus measures but the rally quickly fizzled.

"Still, people have traumatic experience with Chinese equities," said Pictet's Chen. "China used to be called uninvestible and so this deflationary kind of narrative still hasn't been completely dissipated."

Bhatia said his clients are asking more about tactical allocations, an investment strategy that aims to take advantage of trends and economic changes with short-term bets.

"The past ten days have made it very clear that it pays off to have a regionally diversified allocation strategy," said Lilian Haag, a senior portfolio manager at DWS.

data.title: Habitat for Humanity offers up to US$40,000 in grants for innovative youth-led housing solutions in Asia-Pacific
data.description:
MANILA, Mar 14, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - Habitat for Humanity is excited to engage young people in the Asia-Pacific region in creating innovative housing solutions through grants worth up to US$40,000. Through the Habitat Youth Solutions grant, up to 10 young people or youth organizations in the region can each win US$4,000 to start, scale up, or replicate culturally and contextually appropriate projects that help their local communities improve their housing conditions.

"The Asia-Pacific region is not on track to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals, as U.N. data show. With rapid urbanization, climate change, environmental degradation and social inequality, sustainable cities and communities seem like a distant dream," said Liz Satow, Area Vice President for Asia-Pacific, Habitat for Humanity International.

"But there is hope. The youth in Asia-Pacific bring a wealth of exciting and creative ideas, passion, and determination to address these challenges. When governments, civil society and communities join hands with the youth of Asia-Pacific, the partnership creates solutions that are grounded in the reality of their communities, and therefore, can be incredibly effective."

The 13-year Habitat Young Leaders Build campaign attests to the youth's potential and power to advance the cause of affordable housing. Besides the grant, up to 40 shortlisted applicants will convene at the Youth Assembly in the Philippines in mid-2025 to network with experts in housing, urban development and project management, learn from previous grant winners, and ideate with peers from across the region. In addition, winners of the 2025 Habitat Youth Solutions grant will receive mentorship from experts and implementors from Habitat's network of volunteer leaders and development partners.

Through such youth engagement and collaboration, about 4,000 individuals in up to 10 communities in the region will gain access to affordable housing.

Past winners of the 2023 Habitat Youth Solutions included Indonesian organization Gajahlah Kebersihan. They trained 30 community members in manufacturing ventilation blocks from recycled plastics. In six months, 220,000 pieces of plastics were reused as building materials instead of ending up as marine pollutants. About 100 Indonesian students also helped raise awareness about the risks of coastal housing and highlighted the innovative recycling initiative.

"Winning Habitat's Youth Solutions Micro-grant was a game-changer for us. It allowed us to expand our impact, be more involved in sustainable building issues and connect with like-minded people and organizations," said Dicky Alfandy, co-founder of Gajahlah Kebersihan.

Application for the 2025 Habitat Youth Solutions grant is open until April 13, 2025. Interested young leaders can learn more through the official web page.

About Habitat for Humanity

Driven by the vision that everyone needs a decent place to live, Habitat for Humanity found its earliest inspirations as a grassroots movement on an interracial community farm in U.S.A. Since its founding in 1976, the housing organization has grown to become a leading global nonprofit working in more than 70 countries. In the Asia-Pacific region since 1983, Habitat for Humanity has supported millions of people to build or improve a place they can call home. Through financial support, volunteering or adding a voice to support affordable housing, everyone can help families achieve the strength, stability and self-reliance they need to build better lives for themselves. To learn more, donate or volunteer, visit habitat.org/asiapacific.

data.title: Singapore Post Shareholders Vote in Favor of Australian Business Disposal
data.description:
Singapore Post's shareholders voted in favor of the disposal of the company's Australian logistics business, Freight Management Holdings, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Following the conclusion of an extraordinary general meeting, the proposed disposal garnered 99.9% of the votes.

According to the filing, the disposal will allow the group more liquidity and support 'meaningful deleveraging of debt'.

The sale of the business will generate around AU$775.9 million in gross proceeds, which will be utilized for debt repayment and to issue a special dividend along with residual proceeds.

data.title: Hyundai, Toyota reshape hydrogen vehicle market with new models
data.description: Hyundai Motor Co. is set to launch a second-generation hydrogen model, while Toyota Motor Corp. expands its hydrogen-powered lineup to reshape the fuel cell electric vehicle (FCEV) market. According to industry sources on Thursday, the FCEV sector is entering a new phase with the introduction of new models and advancements in third-generation hydrogen fuel cell systems. It has been more than a decade since Hyundai introduced the world’s first mass-produced hydrogen fuel cell vehicle, the Tucson ix FCEV, in 2013, followed by Toyota’s Mirai in 2014. Toyota has been particularly active in recent years. In 2020, it launched the second-generation Mirai with a larger body and extended driving range. The company introduced a hydrogen-powered Crown model in early 2024, marking a shift from dedicated FCEVs to integrating fuel cell technology into a long-established mainstream lineup that also includes internal combustion and hybrid powertrains. Hyundai, which has not released a new hydrogen model or generational update since the first Nexo’s debut in 2018, is set to counter with the second-generation Nexo in 2025. According to energy market research firm SNE Research, Hyundai remained the global FCEV sales leader in 2024, selling 3,836 units of the Nexo and its hydrogen bus, Elec City. However, sales declined 23.5 percent year-on-year, largely due to weak domestic demand amid the absence of a new Nexo model. In response, Hyundai unveiled the second-generation Nexo concept car, Initium, in October 2024. While the FCEV market has stagnated, hydrogen vehicle sales have been growing in China, particularly in the commercial segment, accounting for 55.3 percent of global FCEV sales in 2024 with 7,113 units sold. Hyundai and Toyota have been competing in the hydrogen vehicle market for a decade, but as overall market growth remains a challenge, they are also strengthening cooperation. Hyundai Motor Executive Chair Euisun Chung and Toyota Chair Akio Toyoda met in November at the 2024 World Rally Championship in Japan. Hyundai Motor Group also launched its hydrogen value chain brand, HTWO, in 2024.

data.title: Kakao founder Kim Beom-su to resign as CA Council co-chair
data.description: Kakao Corp. founder Kim Beom-su will step down as co-chair of the Corporate Alignment (CA) Council, its top decision-making body, due to health reasons. The CA Council will now be led solely by Kakao Chief Executive Officer Chung Shin-a. Kakao announced on Thursday that Kim would be resigning from his positions as co-chair of the CA Council and chairman of the Council’s Management Reform Committee. This decision comes after he was recently diagnosed with early-stage bladder cancer and needs to focus on treatment for the time being. Kim returned to the forefront of management in November 2023, when he took on the role of Management Reform Committee chairman. The appointment came amid internal and external challenges including regulatory investigations into allegations of stock price manipulation related to Kakao’s acquisition of SM Entertainment Co. and internal conflicts among executives. In January 2024, he was appointed co-chair of the CA Council alongside then-CEO designate Chung, leading a major structural overhaul of the group by shifting away from its previous decentralized management system. However, his reform efforts came to a halt in July 2024 when he was arrested in connection with the SM acquisition case. Although he was released on bail in October after 101 days in detention, ongoing legal proceedings prevented him from actively engaging in management. With his health concerns nowadding to the situation, he decided to step down from his role as co-chair of the CA Council. With Kim also resigning as chairman of the Management Reform Committee, the committee will be dissolved. Its responsibilities will now be carried forward by various subcommittees, including the Strategy Committee, Responsible Management Committee, ESG Committee, and Brand Committee, which comprise CEOs of key Kakao subsidiaries. However, Kim will continue to serve as the head of Kakao’s Future Initiative Center, which responsible for shaping the group’s vision and long-term strategy. In the meantime, Kakao also announced on Thursday that it will spin off its portal service, Daum, which currently operates as an in-house independent company. The specific timeline and details of the spinoff have not been disclosed yet.

data.title: Hyundai Mobis aims to expand global customers
data.description: South Korea’s Hyundai Mobis Co. has expressed its commitment to developing top-tier technology to become the world’s leading auto parts company. The company announced Thursday that it held a vision declaration event on Wednesday at its research center in Yongin, Gyeonggi Province, attended by around 300 executives and employees, including Chief Executive Officer Lee Gyu-suk. The new vision is to “lead the transformation of the future automotive industry and push the boundaries of innovation to expand in the global market.” Under this vision, Hyundai Mobis has set three key themes: pioneering mobility innovation, expanding global customers, and limitless growth. Pioneering mobility innovation focuses on securing breakthrough technologies. The company aims to strengthen its position in software-defined vehicles (SDVs), human-machine interfaces (HMIs), electric vehicles (EVs), and chassis safety components, delivering differentiated solutions. Expanding global customers targets increasing the share of overseas revenue from the current 10 percent to 40 percent by 2033. To achieve this, Hyundai Mobis will continue refining its business structure with a focus on profitability. Lee emphasized that establishing a vision is only the first step toward meaningful change, calling for a company-wide effort to bring it to fruition.

data.title: Kakao faces restrictions on data use in AI agent project
data.description: Kakao Corp.faced data-use challenges with its artificial intelligence (AI) agent, Kanana, which is being developed in partnership with OpenAI. The challenges come after the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) ruled that OpenAI cannot use personal data from chat conversations. The PIPC announced the results of a Kanana preliminary review on Thursday. Kakao had requested the review due to concerns about the potential inclusion of personal information, such as account and credit card details in chat conversations, as Kanana is designed to leverage both Kakao’s large language model (LLM) and OpenAI’s ChatGPT for enhanced functionality. Kakao announced its strategic partnership with OpenAI to co-develop an AI agent capable of understanding conversation context and providing relevant responses in February 2025. However, the PIPC’s ruling limits how Kanana can use personal data, ensuring that any data handling remains within strict boundaries. Under Korea’s privacy law, businesses may only collect and use personal information when deemed “necessary” for “performing a contract concluded with the data subject or concluding a contract.” If personal data is not required for such purposes, explicit consent from the user is mandatory. The rationale is that since Kakao is responsible for managing user data and OpenAI can only process encrypted data within Kakao’s defined scope, OpenAI would not be permitted to independently store or use the data even if ChatGPT were to process it. A PIPC official confirmed that the commission has required explicit language to ensure that OpenAI can only use the data for tasks directly commissioned by Kakao, not for its own business objectives. This restriction is expected to limit OpenAI’s ability to collect valuable data from Kanana, prompting many industry insiders to speculate that the AI service may eventually be monetized.

data.title: Samsung SDS invests $5.36 mn in Mistral AI
data.description: Samsung SDS made an equity investment in Mistral AI, Europe’s leading independent AI lab,in February 2024 according to reports on Thursday. According to Samsung SDS’s 2024 business report, the company acquired a 0.12 percent stake in the world’s fifth-largest AI company, with a book value of approximately 7.8 billion won ($5.36 million). Samsung SDS is currently testing Mistral AI within its proprietary generative AI platform, FabriX, and will determine its future application after internal review. Founded in 2023, Mistral AI is a French AI startup led by Arthur Mensch, a former Google DeepMind researcher. Samsung SDS said it had invested in Mistral AI as part of a broader technology collaboration effort.

data.title: SK hynix components to be tested in space
data.description: SK hynix Inc.’s DRAM and other domestically-produced components and devices will be launched into space aboard South Korea’s Nuri rocket to undergo performance verification. On Thursday, the Korea Aerospace Administration and the Korea Aerospace Research Institute announced the results of the payload selection for the Domestic Components Space Verification Support Program for the second space verification satellite. The space verification program, which runs for four years from 2024 with a budget of 12 billion won, aims to support the verification of domestically developed electrical and electronic components and semiconductors in space. The program will test whether these components function properly in space and assess their resistance to exposure to cosmic radiation. According to the selection results, SK hynix’s DRAM and flash-memory products, including UFS, will be onboard the Nuri, a three-stage launch vehicle developed by the KARI. Other components manufactured by startups were also selected for assessment, including InceraSolutions’ high-speed precision mirrors, Cosmo Bee’s 1A-class hollow cathode for electric thrusters, and InterGravity Technologies‘ orbital transport ship anti-jamming equipment.

data.title: Cuckoo targets $1.38 bn in sales with business diversification
data.description: Cuckoo, which began as a rice cooker specialist in South Korea is seeing strong results from its business diversification strategy. After strengthening its home appliance lineup with robot vacuum cleaners and air purifiers, the company has recently expanded into new product categories such as kimchi refrigerators. Cuckoo Holdings, which includes rental service provider Cuckoo Homesys and manufacturer Cuckoo Electronics, aims to hit 2 trillion won ($1.38 billion) in total sales in 2025. Cuckoo said on Thursday that Cuckoo Homesys recorded 1.05 trillion won in revenue in 2024, a 10.8 percent increase year-over-year, marking the first time it surpassed 1 trillion won in annual sales. With Cuckoo Holdings generating 594.5 billion won in revenue through the third quarter of 2024, total group sales are projected to reach 1.9 trillion won for the year. Cuckoo’s recent product launches underscore its evolution into a comprehensive home appliance brand. In 2025 to date, the company has introduced a two-person electric rice cooker, a microbial food waste processor, the Restino Euro Top mattress and frame, and a cordless hand blender. Cuckoo responded to increasing competition from Chinese brands by launching four consecutive vacuum cleaner models, starting with the Power Clone Slim, in 2024. The company has been diversifying into kitchen appliances for smaller households, rental-based products, and general home electronics. A Cuckoo official said, “We maintain a broad product lineup, ranging from high-end to budget-friendly options across categories such as rice cookers, water purifiers, induction cooktops, and vacuum cleaners. New product launches are carefully evaluated based on requests from retail distribution channels.” A prime example is Cuckoo’s 133L kimchi refrigerator, launched in 2024 in response to retailer demand for a reliable, affordably priced model in the 600,000 won range. Cuckoo’s leadership believes that if low-cost Chinese products dominate the market, consumer choice will shrink significantly, prompting the company to develop products that align with market demand. To reinforce stability in its new businesses, Cuckoo is doubling down on its expertise in “gourmet appliances,” ensuring that its products support every step of the cooking process. The company offers freezers and kimchi refrigerators for food storage, rice cookers, induction cooktops, microwaves, air fryers, and blenders for cooking alongside dishwashers and food waste processors for post-meal cleanup. This strategy has proven successful, with sales of gourmet appliances - including induction cooktops, microwaves, and food waste processors - rising 39 percent year-over-year in 2024, significantly driving overall appliance revenue. Cuckoo is also exploring business-to-business (B2B) expansion. The company recently signed a water treatment facility supply contract with Lotte International, under which its filtration systems will be used in the production of “no-wash rice” - a product that eliminates the need for rinsing before cooking. Cuckoo’s water purification technology was recognized for maintaining rice quality while effectively removing impurities during processing. The B2B rental business is also growing rapidly, with revenue surging 253 percent year-over-year from January to October 2024. Cuckoo has secured major contracts with hotels like Inspire Resort in Incheon and Lotte Hotel La Seine, as well as CJ Logistics O-NE, NH Nonghyup Bank, public institutions, and military bases.

data.title: Samsung SDI to raise $1.38 bn for EV battery supercycle
data.description: Samsung SDI announced a 2 trillion won ($1.38 billion) rights offering to strengthen its long-term competitiveness and accelerate growth, as the electric vehicle (EV) battery market faces a temporary slowdown in demand. The company’s board of directors approved the capital increase on Friday, saying that the funds will be used for expanding production capacity at its joint venture with General Motors (GM) in the United States, scaling up its Hungary plant, and investing in solid-state battery production facilities in South Korea. The rights offering, which will be conducted via a shareholder allocation followed by a public offering of forfeited shares, will issue 11,821,000 new shares, or a 16.8 percent capital increase. Samsung SDI has set several key dates for the rights offering process. The record date for the new share allocation is April 18th, 2025, while the final offer price will be determined on May 22nd. The subscription period will take place between May 27th and June 3rd, during which priority will be given to employee stock ownership associations and existing shareholders, followed by a general subscription for remaining shares. The new shares will then be listed on June 19th. Samsung SDI said that the capital increase aligns with the EV battery industry’s long-term growth prospects, noting that battery production requires a 2-to-3-year lead time from facility investment to mass production. Global automakers remain committed to long-term electrification strategies despite the slowdown in EV market growth. Market research firms project that the EV battery market will sustain an annual growth rate of around 20 percent between 2025 and 2030. To secure its future growth drivers, Samsung SDI is advancing joint ventures with Stellantis and GM in the United States, expanding Hungary’s production facilities, and investing in next-generation battery technologies, including solid-state batteries and lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries. The company’s capital expenditures have surged almost fourfold from 1.7 trillion won in 2019 to 6.6 trillion won in 2024. While investment is expected to decline slightly in 2025 due to temporary market conditions, Samsung SDI reaffirmed its commitment to securing future technologies and production capacity. Samsung SDI emphasized that the rights offering is a proactive measure to secure funding for long-term investments and maintain financial stability, ensuring market leadership when demand rebounds. The company is also exploring other funding options, including asset utilization, to diversify its capital-raising strategies.

data.title: Hyundai Steel labor, management wage talks collapse again
data.description: The wage negotiations between Hyundai Steel Co.’s labor and management have broken down once again, showing no signs of resolution. While the steel company under South Korea’s auto giant Hyundai Motor Group offered performance-based bonuses at the cost of incurring losses, its labor union continues to demand wage increases that are on par with Hyundai Motor Co., making it difficult for the two sides to reach an agreement. According to industry sources on Thursday, Hyundai Steel’s labor and management resumed wage negotiations later in the day but failed to reach a tentative agreement. The union immediately declared that it would resume partial strikes, citing the company’s lack of additional proposals, and plans to go on strike until next Thursday. Hyundai Steel’s labor and management have been holding collective bargaining negotiations since September 2024 but have been unable to reach an agreement. The union has continued acting, with moves including full strikes and partial or temporary walkouts. In response, the company took a hardline stance by partially locking out certain production lines at its cold-rolling plant in the Dangjin Steelworks. Although the company lifted the lockout on Tuesday and both sides resumed negotiations, the talks fell apart once again. Hyundai Steel’s Suncheon plant also decided to stage a partial strike, with workers stopping work for four hours each day on Friday and Saturday.
